name: docker
description: Container platform for building, shipping, and running distributed applications

subcommands.name: build
subcommands.description: Build an image from a Dockerfile

options.name: --no-cache
options.description: Do not use cache when building the image

options.name: --pull
options.description: Always attempt to pull a newer version of the base image

options.name: --secret
options.description: Secret to expose to the build (id=mysecret,src=./secret)

options.name: --ssh
options.description: SSH agent socket or keys to expose to the build

options.name: --cache-from
options.description: Images to consider as cache sources

options.name: --network
options.description: Set the networking mode for RUN instructions

options.name: --quiet
options.description: Suppress build output and print image ID on success

options.name: --rm
options.description: Remove intermediate containers after build (default true)

options.name: --squash
options.description: Squash newly built layers into a single layer

options.name: --compress
options.description: Compress the build context using gzip

options.name: --iidfile
options.description: Write the image ID to the file

subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: Create and run a new container from an image

options.name: --name
options.description: Assign a name to the container

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Keep STDIN open even if not attached

options.name: --rm
options.description: Automatically remove the container when it exits

options.name: --publish-all
options.description: Publish all exposed ports to random ports

options.name: --env-file
options.description: Read in a file of environment variables

options.name: --network-alias
options.description: Add network-scoped alias for the container

options.name: --memory-swap
options.description: Swap limit equal to memory plus swap

options.name: --gpus
options.description: GPU devices to add (all or device=ID)

options.name: --privileged
options.description: Give extended privileges to the container

options.name: --read-only
options.description: Mount the container's root filesystem as read only

options.name: --device
options.description: Add a host device to the container

options.name: --init
options.description: Run an init process inside the container to forward signals

options.name: --sig-proxy
options.description: Proxy received signals to the process (default true)

options.name: --health-cmd
options.description: Command to run to check health

options.name: --health-retries
options.description: Consecutive failures needed to report unhealthy

options.name: --health-timeout
options.description: Maximum time for one check to run

subcommands.name: ps
subcommands.description: List containers

options.name: --all
options.description: Show all containers (default shows just running)

subcommands.name: images
subcommands.description: List images

subcommands.name: pull
subcommands.description: Download an image from a registry

options.name: --all-tags
options.description: Download all tagged images in the repository

subcommands.name: push
subcommands.description: Upload an image to a registry

options.name: --all-tags
options.description: Push all tags of an image

subcommands.name: exec
subcommands.description: Execute a command in a running container

options.name: --env-file
options.description: Read in a file of environment variables

options.name: --privileged
options.description: Give extended privileges to the command

examples: docker exec -u root mycontainer apt-get update

subcommands.name: stop
subcommands.description: Stop one or more running containers

options.name: --signal
options.description: Signal to send to the container

options.name: --time
options.description: Seconds to wait before killing the container

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Start one or more stopped containers

subcommands.name: restart
subcommands.description: Restart one or more containers

options.name: --signal
options.description: Signal to send to the container

options.name: --time
options.description: Seconds to wait before killing the container

subcommands.name: rm
subcommands.description: Remove one or more containers

options.name: --force
options.description: Force removal of a running container (uses SIGKILL)

options.name: --volumes
options.description: Remove anonymous volumes associated with the container

subcommands.name: rmi
subcommands.description: Remove one or more images

options.name: --no-prune
options.description: Do not delete untagged parent images

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Fetch the logs of a container

options.name: --tail
options.description: Number of lines to show from end of logs

options.name: --since
options.description: Show logs since timestamp or relative (e.g. 10m, 2h)

options.name: --until
options.description: Show logs before a timestamp or relative

options.name: --details
options.description: Show extra details provided to logs

subcommands.name: inspect
subcommands.description: Return low-level information on Docker objects

options.name: --size
options.description: Display total file sizes if the type is container

subcommands.name: network ls
subcommands.description: List networks

subcommands.name: network create
subcommands.description: Create a network

options.name: --gateway
options.description: IPv4 or IPv6 gateway for the subnet

options.name: --ip-range
options.description: Allocate container IP from a sub-range

options.name: --internal
options.description: Restrict external access to the network

subcommands.name: network rm
subcommands.description: Remove one or more networks

options.name: --force
options.description: Do not error if the network does not exist

subcommands.name: network connect
subcommands.description: Connect a container to a network

options.name: --alias
options.description: Add network-scoped alias for the container

examples: docker network connect --alias db my-network postgres-container

subcommands.name: network disconnect
subcommands.description: Disconnect a container from a network

subcommands.name: volume ls
subcommands.description: List volumes

subcommands.name: volume create
subcommands.description: Create a volume

subcommands.name: volume rm
subcommands.description: Remove one or more volumes

options.name: --force
options.description: Force removal of volume (even if in use)

subcommands.name: volume inspect
subcommands.description: Display detailed information on one or more volumes

options.name: --format
options.description: Format output using a Go template

subcommands.name: compose up
subcommands.description: Create and start containers defined in compose file

options.name: --no-build
options.description: Don't build an image, even if it's missing

options.name: --remove-orphans
options.description: Remove containers for services not defined in compose file

options.name: --wait
options.description: Wait for services to be running/healthy

options.name: --wait-timeout
options.description: Maximum duration to wait for project to be running/healthy

options.name: --abort-on-container-exit
options.description: Stop all containers if any container stops

options.name: --no-start
options.description: Don't start the services after creating them

subcommands.name: compose down
subcommands.description: Stop and remove containers, networks defined in compose file

options.name: --volumes
options.description: Remove named volumes declared in the volumes section and anonymous volumes

options.name: --remove-orphans
options.description: Remove containers for services not defined in compose

subcommands.name: compose ps
subcommands.description: List containers for a compose project

subcommands.name: compose logs
subcommands.description: View output from containers

options.name: --tail
options.description: Number of lines to show from end

subcommands.name: compose build
subcommands.description: Build or rebuild services

options.name: --pull
options.description: Always pull a newer version of the image

subcommands.name: compose exec
subcommands.description: Execute a command in a running service container

options.name: --index
options.description: Index of the container if scaled service

examples: docker compose exec db psql -U postgres
docker compose exec -u root web sh
docker compose exec web rails console
docker compose exec -e DEBUG=1 web python manage.py shell

subcommands.name: compose pull
subcommands.description: Pull service images

options.name: --ignore-buildable
options.description: Ignore images that can be built

options.name: --ignore-pull-failures
options.description: Pull what it can, ignore failures

options.name: --include-deps
options.description: Also pull services declared as dependencies

subcommands.name: compose restart
subcommands.description: Restart service containers

subcommands.name: system prune
subcommands.description: Remove unused data (containers, networks, images, optionally volumes)

options.name: --all
options.description: Remove all unused images, not just dangling

subcommands.name: system df
subcommands.description: Show Docker disk usage

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Show detailed information on space usage

subcommands.name: system info
subcommands.description: Display system-wide information

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Log in to a registry

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Log out from a registry

subcommands.name: tag
subcommands.description: Create a tag that refers to a source image

subcommands.name: cp
subcommands.description: Copy files/folders between a container and the local filesystem

options.name: --follow-link
options.description: Always follow symbol link in SRC_PATH

subcommands.name: stats
subcommands.description: Display a live stream of container resource usage statistics

args.name: <container>
args.description: Container name(s) or ID(s) (default: all running)

options.name: --all
options.description: Show all containers (default shows just running)

options.name: --no-stream
options.description: Disable streaming stats and only pull the first result

subcommands.name: top
subcommands.description: Display the running processes of a container

subcommands.name: port
subcommands.description: List port mappings or a specific mapping for the container

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Inspect changes to files or directories on a container's filesystem

subcommands.name: commit
subcommands.description: Create a new image from a container's changes

args.name: <repository[:tag]>
args.description: Repository and tag for new image

options.name: --change
options.description: Apply Dockerfile instruction to the created image

subcommands.name: save
subcommands.description: Save one or more images to a tar archive

options.name: --output
options.description: Write to a file instead of STDOUT

subcommands.name: load
subcommands.description: Load an image from a tar archive or STDIN

subcommands.name: export
subcommands.description: Export a container's filesystem as a tar archive

options.name: --output
options.description: Write to a file instead of STDOUT

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: Import the contents from a tarball to create a filesystem image

args.name: <file|url>
args.description: Tar file path, URL, or - for STDIN

options.name: --change
options.description: Apply Dockerfile instruction to the created image

options.name: --message
options.description: Set commit message for imported image

options.name: --platform
options.description: Set platform if server is multi-platform capable

subcommands.name: buildx build
subcommands.description: Build with BuildKit (extended build capabilities)

options.name: --push
options.description: Push image to registry after build

options.name: --load
options.description: Load the single-platform build result to docker images

options.name: --no-cache
options.description: Do not use cache when building

options.name: --pull
options.description: Always attempt to pull all referenced images

options.name: --secret
options.description: Secret to expose to the build

options.name: --ssh
options.description: SSH agent socket or keys to expose

options.name: --network
options.description: Set the networking mode for RUN instructions

options.name: --iidfile
options.description: Write the image ID to the file

options.name: --metadata-file
options.description: Write build result metadata to the file

examples: docker buildx build --cache-from type=registry,ref=myapp:cache --cache-to type=registry,ref=myapp:cache -t myapp .

subcommands.name: buildx create
subcommands.description: Create a new builder instance

options.name: --append
options.description: Append a node to builder instead of changing it

options.name: --leave
options.description: Remove a node from builder instead of changing it

subcommands.name: buildx use
subcommands.description: Set the current builder instance

options.name: --default
options.description: Set builder as default for current context

subcommands.name: buildx ls
subcommands.description: List builder instances

globalOptions.name: --context
globalOptions.description: Name of the context to use for connecting to the daemon

globalOptions.name: --tlscacert
globalOptions.description: Trust certs signed only by this CA

globalOptions.name: --tlsverify
globalOptions.description: Use TLS and verify the remote

detectors.name: running_containers
detectors.description: Currently running containers with name, image, status, and ports

detectors.name: images
detectors.description: Locally available Docker images with repository, tag, size, and ID

detectors.name: networks
detectors.description: Docker networks with name, driver, and scope

detectors.name: compose_services
detectors.description: Running Docker Compose services and their status

detectors.name: docker_info
detectors.description: Docker daemon server version, OS, CPU, and memory summary

detectors.name: disk_usage
detectors.description: Docker disk usage by resource type

detectors.name: dangling_images
detectors.description: IDs of dangling (untagged) images that can be pruned

detectors.name: buildx_builders
detectors.description: Available BuildKit builder instances and their status
